---
title: "Bebop Setup Kit"
subtitle: "Group tutorial — dev environments, VSCode Remote, and Claude Code on LCRC"
author: "Jonathan Ozik"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    incremental: false
---

## Overview {.smaller}

A collection of **user-space** setup kits for working on Argonne's LCRC **Bebop** cluster. No admin required, everything installs into `$HOME`.

Already available in the shared EMEWS project space (no clone needed):

```
/lcrc/project/EMEWS/bebop_setup_kit
```

| Piece | What it gives you |
| --- | --- |
| `r-quarto-jupyter/` | R 4.5 + Python + Quarto + Jupyter, wired into VSCode |
| `claude-on-lcrc/` | Claude Code (CLI + VSCode) via `argo-shim` |
| `agent-bits/` | Batch scripts to run `argo-shim` on a compute node |

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
The **compute node** is the primary way you'll work. The login node just hosts the persistent `argo-shim` bridge — real analysis runs on a node you request via `agent-bits`.
:::

::: {.notes}
Set the stage: everything is user-space, lives in shared EMEWS space, three building blocks. Emphasize early that day-to-day work happens on a compute node — the login-node setup is one-time plumbing. Rest of the deck walks each one, then live demo.
:::

## `r-quarto-jupyter/` — analysis environment {.smaller}

A reproducible, user-space stack for `.Rmd` / `.qmd` / `.ipynb` work.

- **conda-forge R 4.5** with full graphics (Cairo, png, svglite, ragg, httpgd)
- **Python 3 + Jupyter + IRkernel**
- **Quarto 1.9.38** — renders both `.qmd` and `.Rmd`
- **VSCode** Machine settings + extensions (R, Quarto, Jupyter) for inline plots, Workspace Viewer, kernel pickers

Install (idempotent — safe to re-run):

```bash
ssh bebop
cd /lcrc/project/EMEWS/bebop_setup_kit/r-quarto-jupyter
bash setup_bebop_env.sh
```

Then: VSCode → Remote-SSH to `bebop` → reload → verify with the `test/` smoke files.

## `claude-on-lcrc/` — login-node setup {.smaller}

One-time plumbing: a persistent `argo-shim` bridge that is the *foundation* for the compute-node workflow.

- **`argo-shim`** bridges Bebop to GCE — install in a Python venv (`module load python`)
- Runs in a **persistent tmux session** on a single login node (survives disconnects)
  - Auth via **Duo push** → `✅ All health checks passed`; **note your base port**
- Point local `~/.ssh/config` at the **specific login node** (`b1`…`b5`)
- Install Claude: `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`, then **smoke-test** here

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Login-node Claude is **only** to confirm the bridge works — don't do real work on shared login nodes.
:::

::: {.notes}
Two gotchas to flag live: argo-shim is pinned to one login node, and the base port matters for the compute-node slide. Login-node Claude is verification only.
:::

## `agent-bits/` — Claude on a compute node {.smaller}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**This is the main workflow.** Day-to-day, you run Claude here — on a dedicated DIS condo compute node, tunneled through the login-node `argo-shim`.
:::

- Start a **second** tmux session running a *tunneled* `argo-shim`:
  ```bash
  argo-shim --tunnel --tunnel-port <BASE_PORT+2>
  ```
- Submit a compute node with the wrapper (pass the login node hosting your tunnel):
  ```bash
  /lcrc/project/EMEWS/bebop_setup_kit/agent-bits/submit-argo-shim.sh \
    beboplogin5.lcrc.anl.gov
  ```
- Script waits for the job, then prints the node (also in `~/argo-shim-node.txt`):
  ```
  argo-shim node: dis-00NN
  ```
- Add a local SSH entry (`bcn`) that **ProxyJumps** through Bebop → connect in VSCode

Ports are derived per-user automatically — nothing to set by hand on the compute side.

## Hands-on demonstration {.smaller}

Putting it together — what we'll walk through live:

0. **Pre-requisites** - GCE account, SSH key on LCRC for accessing Argonne GCE
1. **Connect** — VSCode Remote-SSH into Bebop
2. **Analysis env** — open a `.qmd` / `.ipynb`, render a plot inline
3. **argo-shim** — reattach the tmux session, confirm health checks
4. **Quick test** — launch Claude via the star icon on the login node, confirm it works
5. **Compute node (the real workflow)** — submit via `agent-bits`, connect to `bcn`, work here

**Resources**
- Repo: `github.com/jozik/bebop_setup_kit`
- In place on Bebop: `/lcrc/project/EMEWS/bebop_setup_kit`
- Questions: jozik@anl.gov
